source_text:
Slice 1: Governed Exception Receipt Contracts and Plan Source Retention

Status: approved Slice 1 execution input
Authority: source material for typed PlanRow ingestion; execution authority is the
typed PlanRow plus receipts in dev/state, not this temporary source file.
Parent: MP-377 / portable AI governance platform

Core correction:
Bypass is not the feature. Exception -> Repair -> Proof -> Learning is the
feature. A governed exception must become typed repair debt, proof work, plan
or finding linkage, projection updates, and learning that prevents recurrence.
Slice 1 creates receipt contracts, validation, registry visibility, source
retention, and read-only surfaces only. It introduces no bypass execution path.

Architecture alignment:
- devctl is the backend/control-plane entrypoint.
- devctl develop is the universal developer loop composition surface.
- devctl agent, if later added, may only be a thin wrapper over devctl develop.
- SYSTEM_MAP and ConnectivityRegistrySnapshot are the connectivity/projection spine.
- system-picture is the current-state warm-start.
- context-graph / graph-walk / devctl map provide focused navigation.
- SystemCatalog describes what exists.
- AgentDispatchPacket may recommend bounded next action, but is not authority.
- WorkIntakePacket / PlanExpectationPacket route bounded work.
- TypedAction is mutation authority.
- ActionResult / RunRecord / ValidationReceipt are execution proof.
- FindingBacklog and PlatformFindingIngest are canonical finding/debt state.
- dogfood, governance-review, and guard-effectiveness surfaces are learning/adjudication.
- render-surfaces, bridge, dashboard, mobile, slash commands, generated markdown,
  CLAUDE.md, CODEX.md, latest.md, and memory notes are projections or adapters only.
- No generated markdown, bridge text, dashboard row, slash command, or memory note
  may become authority over typed state.

Do not create:
- raw bypass command
- blessed raw git push --no-verify path
- parallel finding system
- parallel event/lifecycle system
- parallel scheduler
- parallel dashboard authority path
- parallel developer-agent authority path
- hand-maintained DEVELOPER_AGENT.md authority file

Allowed:
- raw git push --no-verify may only be imported as historical/manual evidence
  through ManualBypassImportReceipt.
- generated markdown may be created only as projection-only output with provenance.
- devctl exceptions validate and pending are read-only in Slice 1.

Master architecture:
devctl develop
  uses startup-context + session-resume
  reads SYSTEM_MAP / ConnectivityRegistrySnapshot
  reads system-picture
  queries context-graph / graph-walk / devctl map
  routes WorkIntakePacket / PlanExpectationPacket
  executes TypedAction only in future execution slices
  records ActionResult / RunRecord / ValidationReceipt
  manages GovernedExceptionLifecycle in later slices
  proves ResolutionReceipt in later slices
  updates FindingBacklog / dogfood / governance-review
  projects through render-surfaces / bridge / dashboard / mobile / generated MD
  learns through GuardEffectivenessReport / GuardPromotionCandidate / GoldenReplay

Master invariants:
- No developer-loop decision without startup-context, session-resume,
  system-picture, and SYSTEM_MAP/context-connectivity evidence.
- No mutation without TypedAction.
- No exception without explicit typed reason.
- No exception without pending ExceptionReceipt.
- No ExceptionReceipt without linked FindingBacklog entry or planned
  FindingBacklog ingestion hook.
- No Finding without RemediationPacket or explicit waiver.
- No remediation without ValidationPlan.
- No closure without ResolutionReceipt and ClosureProof.
- No repeated exception without GuardEffectivenessReport and escalation.
- No SYSTEM_MAP edge may be hand-maintained when ConnectivityRegistrySnapshot can own it.
- No system-picture section may survive contradiction with canonical typed state.
- No generated markdown, bridge text, dashboard row, slash command, or memory note
  may become authority over typed state.
- No raw git, raw push, bridge prose, dashboard click, or slash command may bypass
  the typed backend.
- For vcs.push, remote-ref and post-push proof remain mandatory.

Governed Exception Lifecycle:
blocked
  -> classified
  -> bounded_auto_repair_attempted
  -> exception_requested
  -> exception_policy_checked
  -> operator_approved
  -> receipt_written_pending
  -> exception_executed
  -> result_verified
  -> finding_opened_or_refreshed
  -> remediation_packet_created
  -> startup_context_projected
  -> assigned_to_agent_or_slice
  -> fix_attempted
  -> validation_plan_rerun
  -> resolution_receipt_written
  -> closure_proof_verified
  -> guard_promotion_or_waiver_recorded
  -> closed

Recurrence:
closed -> repeated_failure_detected -> reopened -> escalated

Endpoint:
The endpoint is not "the push landed." The endpoint is that the original
guarded path passes normally without the exception and the system has proof
that the failure class is fixed or explicitly waived. For the ReviewSnapshot
push case, the exception lifecycle closes only after
python3 dev/scripts/devctl.py push --execute passes later without exception
flags, without raw --no-verify, and with remote-ref/post-push proof.

Exception policy belongs in ProjectGovernance / RepoPack:
- allowed_exception_classes
- forbidden_exception_classes
- required_proof_by_action_kind
- operator_approval_rules
- stale_head_policy
- dirty_worktree_policy
- remote_publish_policy
- security_guard_policy

Allowed exception examples:
- managed_projection_refresh_failure
- generated_surface_drift
- review_snapshot_refresh_failure
- known_false_positive_guard
- transient_lock_after_bounded_retry
- stale_projection_index

Forbidden exception examples:
- security_secret_detection
- missing_subprocess_evidence
- remote_rejection_or_conflict
- destructive_migration
- unreviewed_authored_source_dirt
- stale_head_authorization
- changed_worktree_after_approval
- unknown_executor_result

Auto-repair before exception:
Before allowing an exception, the system should attempt bounded auto-repair for
safe classes such as generated projection refresh, bridge regeneration, system
map/render-surface refresh, ReviewSnapshot cache rebuild, plan index projection
refresh, stale dashboard projection refresh, dogfood summary refresh, context
graph snapshot refresh, typed receipt index rebuild, and known idempotency retry
issues. Auto-repair is not appropriate for security failures, secret findings,
remote conflicts, destructive migrations, auth changes, unreviewed source
changes, policy changes, ambiguous operator intent, or missing subprocess evidence.

Auto-repair flow:
guard fails -> classify failure -> if safe, run bounded auto-repair -> rerun guard
-> if green, write AutoRepairReceipt and continue normally -> if still blocked,
allow typed exception request -> write ExceptionReceipt -> create finding and
remediation packet -> require ResolutionReceipt later.

Typed objects in scope for Slice 1:
- GovernedExceptionLifecycle
- ExceptionReceipt
- ResolutionReceipt
- ExceptionPolicy
- ExceptionClass
- ExceptionLifecycleStatus
- ClosureProof
- AutoRepairReceipt
- ManualBypassImportReceipt
- PlanSourceSnapshot

Typed objects deferred to later slices:
- GuardEffectivenessReport
- GuardFailureRemediationPacket
- GuardPromotionCandidate
- RuntimeAgreementReport
- TypedTransitionSimulation
- ExplainBackReceipt
- GraphContradictionIndex
- SystemMapCompletenessReport
- ValidationPlan execution/proof engine
- ValidationReceipt creation for exception proof

GovernedExceptionLifecycle shape:
schema_version, contract_id/kind, lifecycle_id, status, exception,
resolution, closure_proof, finding_id, planned_finding_ingest_ref,
validation_plan_id, authority_evidence_refs, worktree_safety_evidence_refs,
system_map_contract_ids, developer_loop_refs, learning_refs, projection_refs,
resolution_receipt_id, created_at_utc, and updated_at_utc. This envelope links
receipt, finding, validation, resolution, projection, and learning state without
granting exception execution authority.

ExceptionReceipt shape:
schema_version, contract_id, receipt_id, action_kind, phase, guard_id,
exception_class, operator_reason, head, finding_id, planned_finding_ingest_ref,
worktree_fingerprint, remote, policy_result, authority_evidence_refs,
worktree_safety_evidence_refs, validation_plan_id, execution_status,
remote_ref_verified, post_push_proof_ref, expires_at_utc, and created_at_utc.
The receipt is evidence only. Slice 1 validates it but does not execute it.

ResolutionReceipt shape:
schema_version, contract_id, resolution_id, exception_lifecycle_id, finding_id,
status, root_cause_class, root_cause_summary, fixed_by_commit, changed_files,
validation_receipt_id, action_result_id, dogfood_evidence_id, closure_proof_id,
exception_used, remote_ref_verified, post_push_green, closed_at_utc, and
closure_reason.

Closure requires:
1. The original guard/action pair is identified.
2. The root cause is classified.
3. The fix is linked to a commit or explicit non-code correction.
4. The original command passes without exception.
5. The required ValidationPlan passes.
6. Dogfood/governance-review records the outcome.
7. startup-context, session-resume, system-picture, dashboard, bridge, and
   generated surfaces no longer show stale open debt.
8. Repetition rules are updated.
9. GuardEffectivenessReport, GuardPromotionCandidate, or explicit waiver is recorded.
No proof, no closure.

SYSTEM_MAP / ConnectivityRegistry requirements:
Every new exception object should register into the shared contract registry and
connectivity spine where possible:
- GovernedExceptionLifecycle
- ExceptionReceipt
- ResolutionReceipt
- ClosureProof
- ExceptionPolicy
- ExceptionClass
- ExceptionLifecycleStatus
- AutoRepairReceipt
- ManualBypassImportReceipt
- PlanSourceSnapshot

Each contract should declare, where repo conventions support it:
- contract name and version
- owner
- dataclass/runtime model
- allowed producers and consumers through registry rows
- storage location
- validation function
- fixtures/tests
- projection surfaces
- SYSTEM_MAP / ConnectivityRegistry edges
- context-graph node/edge mapping
- retirement/migration behavior

system-picture requirements:
system-picture may show open exception debt as current state, but cannot close
or mutate it. Expected later projection: open governed exceptions count,
highest severity, blocking mutation yes/no, safe_to_fanout yes/no, next repair
command, proof command, and stale projections.

Focused context requirements:
Later devctl develop should use context-graph / graph-walk / devctl map focused
queries for exception repair rather than reading the whole repo. For the
ReviewSnapshot push case, focused context should include ReviewSnapshot refresh
code, push preflight projection code, managed receipt chain code, push
authorization code, startup push-state projection, docs/check freshness guard,
exception lifecycle receipt, linked finding, validation plan, and recent
dogfood evidence.

DocPolicy / DocRegistry / generated markdown classification:
Exception-facing markdown is never authority. Exception JSON/JSONL/event rows
are authority. SYSTEM_MAP, bridge, CLAUDE.md, CODEX.md, latest.md, dashboard
markdown, slash commands, and similar surfaces are projections/adapters only.
Generated exception projections must carry provenance showing source contract,
source command, source hash, rendered_at, tree hash, and stale/not-stale state.

Authority and capability requirements:
Mutation-class exception receipts must eventually check AuthoritySnapshot,
SessionPosture, CollaborationSession.actor_authorities, ActorAuthorityState,
CapabilityGrantState, actor capability grant, operator mode, lane, remote
control restrictions, and local GUI/prompt availability policy.

Worktree/orphan requirements:
Mutation-class exception receipts must include worktree/orphan evidence such as
orphan inventory report id, orphan snapshot id, checkout inventory id, work
publication ledger id, stash state, sibling repo state, delegated worker roots,
and safe_for_mutation_exception. No mutation-class exception without this evidence.

RuntimeAgreementReport requirements:
Before future exception execution, compare startup-context, session-resume,
AuthoritySnapshot, ControlPlaneReadModel if available, review-channel
status/doctor, dashboard, mobile/status if available, system-picture, SYSTEM_MAP
connectivity summary, context-graph snapshot id, plan_index / PlanRegistry,
push preflight, and current HEAD/worktree/orphan snapshot. No exception if the
system cannot agree what state it is in, unless the exception class is
specifically runtime-agreement repair.

Graph contradiction / authority leak predicates for later:
- exception_without_finding
- finding_without_remediation
- resolution_without_validation
- closed_exception_with_open_finding
- plan_row_without_receipt
- receipt_without_action_result
- push_success_without_remote_ref
- dashboard_clear_but_startup_blocked
- bridge_clear_but_typed_state_blocked
- same_field_advanced_by_two_authorities

Dogfood failure auto-ingest:
Every failed governed command that writes dogfood evidence should either attach
to an existing finding/remediation packet, create a new finding/remediation
packet, or record explicit no-action classification. No failed dogfood row
should remain inert.

PlanRegistry / plan-index rules:
One-off exceptions stay as findings. Repeated exceptions become plan rows.
Suggested escalation: one occurrence creates/refreshes a finding; two
occurrences in seven days escalates priority; three occurrences creates a
PlanRegistry / MASTER_PLAN task; five occurrences blocks non-emergency
exception until repair is scheduled; recurrence after closure reopens finding
and marks prior resolution insufficient.

Plan-source retention rule:
A durable PlanRow may reference expiring review-channel packets, but it must
also carry or link to a durable PlanSourceSnapshot/hash/body so the plan can be
reconstructed after packet expiry. Review-channel packets are transport and
provenance, not the only source. PlanSourceSnapshot must include plan_row_id,
source_kind, source_ref, source_hash/body_hash, captured_at_utc,
source_packet_id if any, packet_expires_at_utc if known, retention_status,
source_integrity_status, source_text, source_summary, and snapshot_path.

Plan-source retention acceptance:
- MP377-P0-EXC-S1 exists in dev/state/plan_index.jsonl.
- Its work_evidence_ids contain plan_source_snapshot:<id>.
- dev/state/plan_source_snapshots.jsonl contains the matching snapshot.
- The matching snapshot source_text contains the full consolidated plan body,
  including master architecture, invariants, lifecycle, SYSTEM_MAP requirements,
  schema/contract rule, Slice 1 deliverables and acceptance, and future slices.
- dev/state/plan_ingestion_receipts.jsonl includes source_snapshot_ids,
  source_snapshot_path, canonical_source_hash, source_packet_expires_at_utc,
  source_retention_status, source_integrity_status, and
  source_integrity_checked_at_utc.
- Validation fails if a packet-sourced or snapshot-referenced PlanRow lacks a
  reconstructable PlanSourceSnapshot with non-empty source_text and valid hash.
- Snapshot writes are idempotent by snapshot_id, or latest valid append-only
  semantics are documented and deterministic.

Schema / contract registry rule:
Do not create one giant universal schema for the whole program.
One program. Many contracts. One registry.
A schema is universal only for one boundary or one contract. Each typed object
crossing a system seam should have its own schema/contract registered in the
shared platform contract registry if one exists. Do not create a parallel
registry. Do not collapse ExceptionReceipt, GovernedExceptionLifecycle,
ResolutionReceipt, AutoRepairReceipt, ManualBypassImportReceipt, PlanRow,
FindingBacklog, ValidationPlan, ValidationReceipt, context graph, and
ConnectivityRegistrySnapshot into one blob schema.

Typed seam registration guard rule:
Whenever a change introduces a new JSON/JSONL artifact, receipt type, packet
type, PlanRow/finding/action-result shape, graph node/edge type, generated
projection surface, devctl command output intended for tools/agents,
cross-language boundary, persistent state file under dev/state or dev/reports,
or schema-like dataclass/model used across subsystems, the system should
require either a contract registration or an explicit no-contract-needed
rationale with owner, scope, and reason. Slice 1 should add the smallest
repo-aligned registration/check or record a blocked seam, not build a full new
guard framework.

Slice 1 deliverables:
1. Typed models:
   - GovernedExceptionLifecycle
   - ExceptionReceipt
   - ResolutionReceipt
   - ExceptionPolicy
   - ExceptionClass
   - ExceptionLifecycleStatus
   - ClosureProof
   - AutoRepairReceipt
   - ManualBypassImportReceipt
   - PlanSourceSnapshot retention for packet/file/body plan sources
2. JSON/JSONL serialization using existing dataclass and JSONL conventions.
3. Fixture/unit tests:
   - valid ExceptionReceipt
   - missing reason
   - empty/generic reason
   - stale/missing HEAD
   - unknown/forbidden exception class
   - missing linked finding or planned finding-ingest hook
   - closed lifecycle without ResolutionReceipt and ClosureProof
   - mutation-class receipt without authority evidence
   - mutation-class receipt without worktree/orphan evidence
   - vcs.push success without remote-ref/post-push proof
   - missing governed-exception store returns empty pending list
   - malformed governed-exception store fails visibly
   - packet-sourced PlanRows fail without reconstructable PlanSourceSnapshot
   - PlanSourceSnapshot writes are deterministic/idempotent by snapshot_id
4. Registry integration:
   - platform contract rows for each Slice 1 contract
   - ConnectivityRegistrySnapshot / SYSTEM_MAP visibility through existing registry paths
   - context-graph node discoverability through existing registry-derived graph paths
   - generated projection hook only if existing render-surface pattern supports it cleanly
5. Read-only CLI if command routing supports it:
   - python3 dev/scripts/devctl.py exceptions validate <path>
   - python3 dev/scripts/devctl.py exceptions pending --format json
6. Optional develop integration:
   - devctl develop --next may show open exception debt only if existing develop
     shell supports read/composition-only consumption without new authority.

Slice 1 hard rules:
- No actual exception execution.
- No raw bypass execution.
- No git push --no-verify path.
- No mutation path.
- No dashboard/slash/bridge state writes.
- No generated markdown as source of truth.
- No parallel lifecycle/event/registry/finding system.
- No exception request/repair/prove/close execution paths beyond read-only validate/pending.

Slice 1 acceptance:
1. A valid ExceptionReceipt fixture validates.
2. Missing reason fails validation.
3. Empty/generic reason fails validation.
4. Missing linked finding or planned finding-ingest hook fails validation.
5. Closed lifecycle without ResolutionReceipt and ClosureProof fails validation.
6. Unknown exception class fails validation.
7. Mutation-class receipt without authority evidence fails validation.
8. Mutation-class receipt without worktree/orphan evidence fails validation.
9. vcs.push success without remote-ref/post-push proof is not considered successful.
10. Exception contracts appear in SYSTEM_MAP / ConnectivityRegistrySnapshot or an explicit blocked seam is recorded.
11. devctl exceptions pending --format json can show open lifecycle rows and missing store returns empty.
12. devctl exceptions validate reports fixture failures.
13. PlanSourceSnapshot proves MP377-P0-EXC-S1 is reconstructable after packet expiry.
14. Tests and relevant guards pass.
15. No raw git bypass path is introduced.
16. No generated markdown is treated as source of truth.

Future Slice 2: Exception request path, still no execution.
Add devctl exceptions request with action-kind, phase, guard, exception-class,
reason, and scope. It validates explicit reason, captures HEAD/worktree
fingerprint, captures AuthoritySnapshot / SessionPosture if available, captures
OrphanSnapshot / CheckoutInventory if available, runs RuntimeAgreementReport if
available, creates pending ExceptionReceipt, creates or stages FindingBacklog
ingestion, shows pending item, and refuses execution.

Future Slice 3: FindingBacklog + remediation routing.
Every pending exception creates or refreshes a stable finding. The finding links
back to the receipt and requires remediation: fix root cause, rerun original
command without exception, and write ResolutionReceipt. devctl develop --next
can then see open exception debt and recommend repair/proof.

Future Slice 4: ResolutionReceipt proof engine.
Add devctl exceptions prove <lifecycle-id>. It reruns the original command
without exception and writes proof only if the original path passes. For
ReviewSnapshot push, python3 dev/scripts/devctl.py push --execute must pass
without exception flags, without --no-verify, and with remote-ref/post-push proof.

Future Slice 5: Controlled one-shot execution path.
Only after Slices 1 through 4 pass. Execution may be allowed only if a pending
receipt already exists, policy allows the class, HEAD/worktree/authority/orphan
evidence still matches, runtime agreement is green or explicitly under repair,
typed transition simulation does not predict a dead end, and vcs.push still
requires remote-ref verification.

Required later ReviewSnapshot end-to-end scenario:
1. devctl push --execute fails during ReviewSnapshot refresh.
2. RuntimeAgreementReport and system-picture classify the failure.
3. bounded auto-repair is attempted.
4. operator approves one-shot exception.
5. ExceptionReceipt is written before execution.
6. canonical TypedAction(action_kind=vcs.push) executes.
7. remote ref is verified.
8. FindingBacklog, RemediationPacket, ValidationPlan, dogfood, system-picture,
   SYSTEM_MAP, context-graph, startup/session/dashboard/bridge projections update.
9. devctl develop --next routes next session to repair the exception.
10. fix lands.
11. devctl exceptions prove reruns the original governed command without exception.
12. ResolutionReceipt closes only after projection clearing and proof.
13. recurrence reopens/escalates and updates GuardEffectivenessReport.

source_text:
Slice 1: Governed Exception Receipt Contracts and Plan Source Retention

Status: approved Slice 1 execution input.
Authority: source material for typed PlanRow ingestion. Durable execution
authority is the typed PlanRow, PlanIntentIngestionReceipt, PlanSourceSnapshot,
contract registry rows, tests, and read-only command output in repo-owned typed
state. This temporary source file is not authority after ingestion.
Parent: MP-377 / portable AI governance platform.

Master architecture

devctl is the backend/control-plane entrypoint. devctl develop is the universal
developer-loop composition surface. devctl agent, if it is added later, may only
be a thin wrapper over devctl develop. SYSTEM_MAP and ConnectivityRegistrySnapshot
are the connectivity and projection spine. system-picture is current-state
warm-start evidence. context-graph, graph-walk, and devctl map provide focused
navigation. SystemCatalog describes what exists. AgentDispatchPacket can
recommend bounded next action, but it is not authority. WorkIntakePacket and
PlanExpectationPacket route bounded work. TypedAction is future mutation
authority. ActionResult, RunRecord, ValidationPlan, ValidationReceipt, dogfood,
governance-review, GuardEffectivenessReport, GuardPromotionCandidate, and golden
replay evidence provide proof and learning. render-surfaces, bridge, dashboard,
mobile, slash commands, generated markdown, CLAUDE.md, CODEX.md, latest.md, and
memory notes are projections or adapters only.

Master invariant

- No developer-loop decision without startup-context, session-resume,
  system-picture, and SYSTEM_MAP/connectivity evidence.
- No mutation without TypedAction.
- No exception without explicit typed reason.
- No exception without pending ExceptionReceipt.
- No ExceptionReceipt without linked FindingBacklog entry or planned
  FindingBacklog ingestion hook.
- No Finding without remediation packet or explicit waiver.
- No remediation without ValidationPlan.
- No closure without ResolutionReceipt and ClosureProof.
- No repeated exception without GuardEffectivenessReport and escalation.
- No SYSTEM_MAP edge may be hand-maintained when ConnectivityRegistrySnapshot
  can own it.
- No system-picture section may survive contradiction with canonical typed state.
- No generated markdown, bridge text, dashboard row, slash command, or memory
  note may become authority over typed state.
- No raw git, raw push, bridge prose, dashboard click, or slash command may
  bypass the typed backend.
- For vcs.push, remote-ref and post-push proof remain mandatory.

Do not create

- raw bypass command
- blessed raw git push --no-verify path
- parallel finding system
- parallel event/lifecycle system
- parallel scheduler
- parallel dashboard authority path
- parallel developer-agent authority path
- hand-maintained DEVELOPER_AGENT.md authority file

Allowed historical evidence:
- raw git push --no-verify may only be imported as historical/manual evidence
  through ManualBypassImportReceipt in a future slice.
- generated markdown may be created only as projection-only output with
  provenance.
- devctl exceptions validate and devctl exceptions pending are read-only in
  Slice 1.

Plan agreement and typed-state ingestion protocol

The full plan must be durable typed source material. A PlanRow must not depend
on an expiring review-channel packet. Review-channel packets can be transport
and evidence, but they cannot be the only source. The durable chain for
MP377-P0-EXC-S1 is:

dev/active/MASTER_PLAN.md human projection
-> dev/state/plan_index.jsonl PlanRow execution state
-> dev/state/plan_ingestion_receipts.jsonl PlanIntentIngestionReceipt proof
-> dev/state/plan_source_snapshots.jsonl PlanSourceSnapshot retained source
-> platform-contract registry, ConnectivityRegistrySnapshot, SYSTEM_MAP, and
   context-graph visibility.

PlanSourceSnapshot must prove full-plan retention, not just non-empty retention.
For MP377-P0-EXC-S1, validation must fail if the matching source_text is only a
short summary, the addendum alone, or just the Slice 1 title. The retained
source_text must include architecture, invariants, lifecycle, schemas/contracts,
retention rules, Slice 1 acceptance, and future slices. Obvious source typos are
corrected in this canonical retained body: Slice 17 -> Slice 1,
governed_exception_lifecycle, forbidden_exception_classes,
transient_lock_after_bounded_retry, vcs.push success, HEAD/worktree still match,
and projection_only.

Discovery before editing

Slice 1 starts by discovering the existing platform-contract registry,
ConnectivityRegistrySnapshot/SYSTEM_MAP builder, context-graph typed-contract
nodes, JSONL conventions, devctl command routing, develop ingestion path, and
guard/check inventory. If a seam exists, reuse it. If a seam is missing or
incomplete, record a blocked integration note or existing AutomationDebt /
PlanReviewFinding convention and proceed only with the safe read-only foundation.
Do not build a second registry, lifecycle store, event system, scheduler, or
dashboard authority path.

Governed Exception Lifecycle concept

Bypass is not the feature. Exception -> Repair -> Proof -> Learning is the
feature. A governed exception must become typed repair debt, proof work, plan or
finding linkage, projection updates, and learning that prevents recurrence.
Slice 1 creates receipt contracts, validation, registry visibility,
PlanSourceSnapshot retention, and read-only CLI surfaces only. It introduces no
exception execution path.

Core lifecycle

blocked
-> classified
-> bounded_auto_repair_attempted
-> exception_requested
-> exception_policy_checked
-> operator_approved
-> receipt_written_pending
-> exception_executed
-> result_verified
-> finding_opened_or_refreshed
-> remediation_packet_created
-> startup_context_projected
-> assigned_to_agent_or_slice
-> fix_attempted
-> validation_plan_rerun
-> resolution_receipt_written
-> closure_proof_verified
-> guard_promotion_or_waiver_recorded
-> closed

Recurrence

closed -> repeated_failure_detected -> reopened -> escalated. Recurrence after
closure reopens the finding, marks the previous resolution insufficient, and
updates guard-effectiveness / promotion evidence or an explicit waiver.

ReviewSnapshot push case

The endpoint is not "the push landed." The endpoint is that the original guarded
path passes normally without exception flags and the system proves that the
failure class is fixed or explicitly waived. For the ReviewSnapshot push case,
closure requires python3 dev/scripts/devctl.py push --execute to pass later
without exception flags, without raw --no-verify, and with remote-ref/post-push
proof.

Exception policy belongs in ProjectGovernance / RepoPack

Exception policy is repo-pack/project-governance data, not provider prose or
bridge text. Policy fields include allowed_exception_classes,
forbidden_exception_classes, required_proof_by_action_kind,
operator_approval_rules, stale_head_policy, dirty_worktree_policy,
remote_publish_policy, and security_guard_policy.

Allowed examples:
- managed_projection_refresh_failure
- generated_surface_drift
- review_snapshot_refresh_failure
- known_false_positive_guard
- transient_lock_after_bounded_retry
- stale_projection_index

Forbidden examples:
- security_secret_detection
- missing_subprocess_evidence
- remote_rejection_or_conflict
- destructive_migration
- unreviewed_authored_source_dirt
- stale_head_authorization
- changed_worktree_after_approval
- unknown_executor_result

Auto-repair before exception

Before allowing an exception, the system should attempt bounded auto-repair for
safe classes such as generated projection refresh, bridge regeneration,
system-map/render-surface refresh, ReviewSnapshot cache rebuild, plan-index
projection refresh, stale dashboard projection refresh, dogfood summary refresh,
context-graph snapshot refresh, typed receipt index rebuild, and known
idempotency retry issues. Auto-repair is not appropriate for security failures,
secret findings, remote conflicts, destructive migrations, auth changes,
unreviewed source changes, policy changes, ambiguous operator intent, or missing
subprocess evidence.

Auto-repair flow:
guard fails -> classify failure -> if safe, run bounded auto-repair -> rerun
guard -> if green, write AutoRepairReceipt and continue normally -> if still
blocked, allow typed exception request -> write ExceptionReceipt -> create
finding and remediation packet -> require ResolutionReceipt later.

Core typed objects

Slice 1 registers and validates:
- GovernedExceptionLifecycle
- ExceptionReceipt
- ResolutionReceipt
- ExceptionPolicy
- ExceptionClass
- ExceptionLifecycleStatus
- ClosureProof
- AutoRepairReceipt
- ManualBypassImportReceipt
- PlanSourceSnapshot

Deferred typed objects:
- GuardEffectivenessReport
- GuardFailureRemediationPacket
- GuardPromotionCandidate
- RuntimeAgreementReport
- TypedTransitionSimulation
- ExplainBackReceipt
- GraphContradictionIndex
- SystemMapCompletenessReport
- ValidationPlan execution/proof engine
- ValidationReceipt creation for exception proof

GovernedExceptionLifecycle shape

schema_version, contract_id/kind, lifecycle_id, status, exception, resolution,
closure_proof, finding_id, planned_finding_ingest_ref, validation_plan_id,
authority_evidence_refs, worktree_safety_evidence_refs, system_map_contract_ids,
developer_loop_refs, learning_refs, projection_refs, resolution_receipt_id,
created_at_utc, and updated_at_utc. The envelope links receipt, finding,
validation, resolution, projection, and learning state without granting
exception execution authority.

ExceptionReceipt shape

schema_version, contract_id, receipt_id, action_kind, phase, guard_id,
exception_class, operator_reason, head, finding_id, planned_finding_ingest_ref,
worktree_fingerprint, remote, policy_result, authority_evidence_refs,
worktree_safety_evidence_refs, validation_plan_id, execution_status,
remote_ref_verified, post_push_proof_ref, expires_at_utc, and created_at_utc.
The receipt is evidence only. Slice 1 validates it but does not execute it.

ResolutionReceipt shape

schema_version, contract_id, resolution_id, exception_lifecycle_id, finding_id,
status, root_cause_class, root_cause_summary, fixed_by_commit, changed_files,
validation_receipt_id, action_result_id, dogfood_evidence_id, closure_proof_id,
exception_used, remote_ref_verified, post_push_green, closed_at_utc, and
closure_reason.

Closure requires

1. The original guard/action pair is identified.
2. The root cause is classified.
3. The fix is linked to a commit or explicit non-code correction.
4. The original command passes without exception.
5. The required ValidationPlan passes.
6. Dogfood/governance-review records the outcome.
7. startup-context, session-resume, system-picture, dashboard, bridge, and
   generated surfaces no longer show stale open debt.
8. Repetition rules are updated.
9. GuardEffectivenessReport, GuardPromotionCandidate, or explicit waiver is
   recorded.

No proof, no closure.

SYSTEM_MAP / ConnectivityRegistry requirements

Every new exception object should register into the shared contract registry and
connectivity spine where possible:
- GovernedExceptionLifecycle
- ExceptionReceipt
- ResolutionReceipt
- ClosureProof
- ExceptionPolicy
- ExceptionClass
- ExceptionLifecycleStatus
- AutoRepairReceipt
- ManualBypassImportReceipt
- PlanSourceSnapshot

Each contract should declare, where repo conventions support it: contract name,
version, owner, dataclass/runtime model, allowed producers, allowed consumers,
storage location, validation function, fixtures/tests, projection surfaces,
SYSTEM_MAP / ConnectivityRegistry edges, context-graph node/edge mapping, and
retirement/migration behavior.

system-picture requirements

system-picture must show open exception debt as current state, but cannot close
or mutate it. Future projection should include open governed exception count,
highest severity, blocking mutation yes/no, safe_to_fanout yes/no, next repair
command, proof command, and stale projections.

DocPolicy / DocRegistry / generated MD classification

Exception-facing markdown is never authority. Exception JSON/JSONL/event rows
are authority. SYSTEM_MAP, bridge, CLAUDE.md, CODEX.md, latest.md, dashboard
markdown, slash commands, and similar surfaces are projections/adapters only.
Generated exception projections must carry provenance and be classified as
generated_report or compatibility_projection with projection_only authority,
canonical_source pointing to GovernedExceptionLifecycle or relevant typed state,
render command, staleness policy, and retention policy.

SurfaceProvenance requirement

Every generated MD/JSON projection should attach provenance with snapshot_id,
source_contract, source_command, source_hash, rendered_at, tree_hash, and
projection_stale. Slice 1 only adds generated projection hooks if existing
render-surface patterns support them cleanly; generated markdown is never the
source of truth.

Authority and capability requirements

Mutation-class exception receipts must eventually check AuthoritySnapshot,
SessionPosture, CollaborationSession.actor_authorities if available,
ActorAuthorityState if available, CapabilityGrantState if available, actor
capability grant, operator mode, lane, remote_control restrictions, and local
GUI/prompt availability policy. Slice 1 validates that mutation-class receipts
carry authority evidence refs; it does not decide or execute mutation.

Worktree/orphan requirements

Mutation-class exception receipts must include worktree/orphan evidence such as
orphan inventory report id, orphan snapshot id, checkout inventory id,
work_publication_ledger id, stash state, sibling repo state, delegated worker
roots, and safe_for_mutation_exception. No mutation-class exception without this
evidence. Slice 1 validates evidence refs only; later slices attach live
OrphanInventoryReport / OrphanSnapshot / CheckoutInventory /
WorkPublicationLedger evidence.

RuntimeAgreementReport requirements

Before future exception execution, compare startup-context, session-resume,
AuthoritySnapshot, ControlPlaneReadModel if available, review-channel
status/doctor, dashboard, mobile/status if available, system-picture, SYSTEM_MAP
connectivity summary, context-graph snapshot id, plan_index / PlanRegistry, push
preflight, and current HEAD/worktree/orphan snapshot. No exception if the system
cannot agree what state it is in, unless the exception class is specifically
runtime-agreement repair.

Graph contradiction / authority leak detection

Later graph predicates should cover exception_without_finding,
finding_without_remediation, resolution_without_validation,
closed_exception_with_open_finding, plan_row_without_receipt,
receipt_without_action_result, push_success_without_remote_ref,
dashboard_clear_but_startup_blocked, bridge_clear_but_typed_state_blocked, and
same_field_advanced_by_two_authorities.

Dogfood failure auto-ingest

Every failed governed command that writes dogfood evidence should either attach
to an existing finding/remediation packet, create a new finding/remediation
packet, or record explicit no-action classification. No failed dogfood row
should remain inert.

PlanRegistry / plan-index rules

One-off exceptions stay as findings. Repeated exceptions become plan rows.
Suggested escalation: one occurrence creates/refreshes a finding; two
occurrences in seven days escalates priority; three occurrences creates a
PlanRegistry / MASTER_PLAN task; five occurrences blocks non-emergency exception
until repair is scheduled; recurrence after closure reopens the finding and
marks prior resolution insufficient.

Protected retention

Exception receipts, resolution receipts, dogfood evidence, validation receipts,
graph proof, contradiction snapshots, golden replay fixtures, PlanSourceSnapshot
rows, and plan-ingestion receipts should follow protected retention policy. A
PlanSourceSnapshot is the durable full source body that lets MP377-P0-EXC-S1 be
reconstructed after review-channel packet expiry.

Swarm/fanout safety

Open exception debt affects fanout policy. An open P0 exception blocks mutable
fanout unless explicitly waived. An open push/commit exception blocks
publish-class worker packets. An open runtime-agreement exception blocks
automation dispatch. Read-only review may continue when safe_to_fanout=false.
Worker packets must include graph-scope proof and validation requirements.
Slice 1 records the contracts but does not write dashboard, bridge, slash, or
fanout state.

Focused context requirement

Later devctl develop should use context-graph / graph-walk / devctl map focused
queries for exception repair instead of reading the whole repo. For the
ReviewSnapshot push case, focused context should include ReviewSnapshot refresh
code, push preflight projection code, managed receipt chain code, push
authorization code, startup push-state projection, docs/check freshness guard,
the exception lifecycle receipt, linked finding, validation plan, and recent
dogfood evidence.

First executable implementation

Implement Slice 1 only:
- PlanSourceSnapshot retention/proof
- governed exception contracts
- validation
- registry/SYSTEM_MAP/context-graph visibility
- read-only exceptions pending/validate CLI
- tests and guards

Do not expand into exception execution. Do not add request, repair, prove,
close, import-manual, raw bypass, git push --no-verify, mutation, dashboard
write, bridge write, slash-command write, or generated markdown authority.

Slice 1 acceptance

1. A valid ExceptionReceipt fixture validates.
2. Missing reason fails validation.
3. Empty/generic reason fails validation.
4. Missing linked finding or planned finding-ingest hook fails validation.
5. Closed lifecycle without ResolutionReceipt and ClosureProof fails validation.
6. Unknown exception class fails validation.
7. Mutation-class receipt without authority evidence fails validation.
8. Mutation-class receipt without worktree/orphan evidence fails validation.
9. vcs.push success without remote-ref/post-push proof is not considered
   successful.
10. Exception contracts appear in SYSTEM_MAP / ConnectivityRegistrySnapshot, or
    an explicit blocked-integration note explains the missing seam.
11. devctl exceptions pending --format json can show open lifecycle rows and
    missing store returns empty.
12. devctl exceptions validate reports fixture failures.
13. PlanSourceSnapshot proves MP377-P0-EXC-S1 is reconstructable after packet
    expiry.
14. Tests and relevant guards pass.
15. No raw git bypass path is introduced.
16. No generated markdown is treated as source of truth.

Future slices

Slice 2: Exception request path, still no execution. Add devctl exceptions
request with action-kind, phase, guard, exception-class, reason, and scope. It
validates explicit reason, captures HEAD/worktree fingerprint, captures
AuthoritySnapshot / SessionPosture if available, captures OrphanSnapshot /
CheckoutInventory if available, runs RuntimeAgreementReport if available,
creates pending ExceptionReceipt, creates or stages FindingBacklog ingestion,
shows pending item, and refuses execution.

Slice 3: FindingBacklog + remediation routing. Every pending exception creates
or refreshes a stable finding. The finding links back to the receipt and
requires remediation: fix root cause, rerun original command without exception,
and write ResolutionReceipt. devctl develop --next can then see open exception
debt and recommend repair/proof.

Slice 4: ResolutionReceipt proof engine. Add devctl exceptions prove
<lifecycle-id>. It reruns the original command without exception and writes
proof only if the original path passes. For ReviewSnapshot push,
python3 dev/scripts/devctl.py push --execute must pass without exception flags,
without --no-verify, and with remote-ref/post-push proof.

Slice 5: Controlled one-shot execution path. Only after Slices 1 through 4 pass.
Execution may be allowed only if a pending receipt already exists, policy allows
the class, HEAD/worktree still match, authority/orphan evidence still matches,
runtime agreement is green or explicitly under repair, typed transition
simulation does not predict a dead end, and vcs.push still requires remote-ref
verification.

Schema / contract registry rule

Do not create one giant universal schema for the whole program. One program.
Many contracts. One registry. A schema is universal only for one boundary or one
contract. Each typed object crossing a system seam should have its own
schema/contract registered in the shared platform contract registry if one
exists. Do not create a parallel registry. Do not collapse ExceptionReceipt,
GovernedExceptionLifecycle, ResolutionReceipt, AutoRepairReceipt,
ManualBypassImportReceipt, PlanRow, FindingBacklog, ValidationPlan,
ValidationReceipt, context graph, and ConnectivityRegistrySnapshot into one blob
schema.

Each contract should declare, where repo conventions support it: contract name,
version, owner, dataclass/model, allowed producers, allowed consumers, storage
location, validation function, fixtures/tests, projection surfaces, SYSTEM_MAP /
ConnectivityRegistry edges, context-graph node/edge mapping, and
retirement/migration behavior.

Contract registration guard / schema registration guard

Whenever a change introduces a new JSON/JSONL artifact, receipt type, packet
type, PlanRow/finding/action-result shape, graph node or edge type, generated
projection surface, devctl command output intended for another tool/agent,
cross-language boundary, persistent state file under dev/state or dev/reports,
or schema-like dataclass/model used across subsystems, the system should require
either a contract registration in the existing registry/platform-contract
system or an explicit no-contract-needed rationale with owner, scope, and
reason. The guard should fail or warn when a new artifact has no contract
registration, a new receipt/packet/finding/action/result type lacks
schema/fixtures, a new graph node/edge type lacks context-graph mapping, a
generated projection lacks SurfaceProvenance, a new contract lacks
producer/consumer edges in SYSTEM_MAP / ConnectivityRegistrySnapshot, a new
contract lacks valid/invalid fixtures, or a contract changes without schema
version, migration, or compatibility note. Slice 1 adds the smallest safe
registration/check or blocked-integration note through existing seams; it does
not build the whole guard system.

source_text: Semantic-link/ZGraph architecture review accepted the hybrid boundary: do not build a parallel ZGraph authority store. Governed exception lifecycle and receipt rows should later be materialized as generated ContextGraphSnapshot nodes and edges from typed state. ContractSpec semantic cross-link metadata is canonical; comments and typing.Annotated are documentation/prototype only. Slice 1B reads dev/state/governed_exception_lifecycles.jsonl, emits lifecycle/receipt/proof nodes with canonical_pointer_ref/provenance_ref back to typed JSONL rows, emits edges using existing edge kinds such as contains, finding_blocks, scoped_by, policy_scopes, receipt_proves, related_to, and routes_to, and keeps the graph projection read-only. It should not add exception execution, repair/prove/close mutation, dashboard writes, bridge writes, slash writes, or Z-ref compression. Acceptance: context-graph exposes governed exception lifecycle and receipt row nodes, semantic cross-links resolve through ContractSpec metadata and target resolvers, snapshot diff works without a second graph store, and missing target resolution becomes PlanReviewFinding/AutomationDebt instead of guessed authority.

It should not implement inferred-property engines, Z-ref compression, graph contradiction gates, mutation/fanout blocking, exception execution, or generated markdown authority. Acceptance: graph-walk can show exception -> finding/planned ingest -> validation plan -> resolution receipt -> closure proof paths when typed rows exist, missing links are reported as explicit unresolved semantic edges, and all output remains projection-only over typed state.

source_text: Operator architecture finding: packet inboxes and packet attention must be addressed by actor role plus exact session when session-scoped, not by provider name. Legacy to_agent/from_agent fields are compatibility delivery labels only; authority comes from target_role, target_session_id, actor authority, capability grants, and packet lifecycle disposition. Single-agent mode must still inspect relevant reviewer and implementer packet debt before making scoped decisions.

source_text: Acceptance correction for GovernedExceptionLifecycle and semantic-link work: green unit tests and static projections are not enough to claim the system works. Before governed exception lineage, semantic cross-links, or graph traversal are considered end-to-end accepted, run a live Codex plus Claude dogfood proof with two phases: Codex implementer and Claude reviewer, then Claude implementer and Codex reviewer. Each phase must prove role/session packet routing, targeted packet observation, ACK or disposition, apply with PacketGuardAttestation when applicable, exceptions request remaining execution_status=not_executed, pending lifecycle visibility, semantic cross-link projection, and check_multi_agent_sync passing after the phase. Dogfood records must include status, doctor, history, packet lifecycle, exception, and guard outputs. This is an acceptance gate and does not widen Slice 1 implementation beyond contracts, validation, registry visibility, and semantic metadata.

source_text: Typed guard-deferral lifecycle future slice. Scope: add GuardDeferralReceipt and QualityClosureReceipt for cleanup-grade guard debt only. Non-deferrable safety proof authority gates cannot be deferred: security, raw bypass, exception execution exposure, missing TypedAction, missing remote-ref proof, missing PlanSourceSnapshot, missing authority contract registration, or failing Slice 1 acceptance tests. Acceptance: deferrable checks are policy-classified; deferral requires reason, target path, owner, follow-up PlanRow FindingBacklog or AutomationDebt, required rerun command, expiry, SYSTEM_MAP and system-picture visibility, and closure proof by rerunning the deferred guard; repeated deferral escalates. This is future work and must not change MP377-P0-EXC-S1 checkpoint behavior.

source_summary: The operator requirement is to stop treating green unit tests as enough for major governance features. After each major feature reaches a coherent green point, the system must physically exercise the real devctl and review-channel workfl...
source_text:
The operator requirement is to stop treating green unit tests as enough for major governance features. After each major feature reaches a coherent green point, the system must physically exercise the real devctl and review-channel workflows, including live Codex plus Claude role-swapped dogfood when collaboration behavior is the feature.

The architecture is not a bypass and not a skip-check mechanism. It is graph-scoped guard cadence and validation scheduling. The system should schedule checks intelligently: cheap safety and authority checks run immediately, feature-local tests run after coherent chunks, subsystem architecture checks run when the edited subsystem is stable, closure checks run before plan status changes or checkpoint, and whole-system cleanup becomes separate plan work after the feature lands.

Tier 0 checks always run immediately and are never deferrable: syntax and import failures, command parser correctness, public command exposure rules, read-only versus mutation boundaries, raw bypass detection, security and secret checks, authority and projection boundaries, generated markdown authority leaks, missing typed receipts for mutation, missing remote-ref proof for push success, and stale HEAD for mutation-class receipts.

Tier 1 checks are local feature checks tied to the current slice, such as governed exception receipt tests, PlanSourceSnapshot tests, exceptions pending and validate command tests, platform contract registration tests, SYSTEM_MAP visibility tests, and direct command smoke checks.

Tier 2 checks are subsystem architecture checks that should run at stabilization points instead of after every small edit when that would force premature local refactors. Examples include full context-graph tests, contract connectivity, platform contract closure, code-shape checks, python dict schema checks, structural similarity warnings, and broad subsystem performance or architecture checks.

Tier 3 checks are full slice closure checks. Before closure, checkpoint, push, or plan status advancement, all deferred or batched guards must run, physical command smoke must be recorded, platform-contracts and system-map output must be inspected, PlanSourceSnapshot proof must be reconstructed, unsafe public commands must remain absent, and any touched context-graph path must pass full context-graph verification.

Tier 4 cleanup is whole-system architecture cleanup after feature delivery. It should become its own PlanRow or automation-debt item instead of widening the active feature slice.

Guard cadence should use ContextGraphSnapshot, ContractSpec.cross_links, runtime contract ownership, touched files, and command catalog metadata to infer impacted contracts, projections, readers, and sufficient checks. If governed exception receipts and platform contract rows are touched, the required set includes governed exception runtime tests, exceptions command tests, platform contract tests, system-map tests, and contract connectivity. If context graph query code is touched, the required set includes full context graph tests, code-shape, and dict schema checks.

A deferred quality check must produce typed evidence such as GuardCadencePolicy, ValidationSchedule, GuardRunPlan, GuardRunRecord, DeferredGuardReceipt, QualityDebtReceipt, or GuardBatchReceipt. The receipt must include guard id, scope, reason, owner, affected paths, risk class, allowed-until boundary, required rerun command, follow-up PlanRow or FindingBacklog or AutomationDebt link, and closure proof. No guard can disappear without a receipt.

The physical dogfood experiment must compare always-run mode against staged cadence mode. Always-run mode runs code-shape, dict schema, full context graph, and contract connectivity after every meaningful edit. Staged cadence mode runs cheap safety checks immediately, feature tests after coherent chunks, architecture guards at stabilization points, and the full closure bundle before checkpoint. Metrics must include elapsed time, failures caught, refactor churn, false positives, late failures, files touched outside slice scope, final code quality, and whether developer flow was blocked or accelerated.

For the current governed exception and packet routing work, do not implement this full cadence system inside MP377-P0-EXC-S1. Add this as a future typed row. Current EXC-S1 still must pass all Slice 1 guards before closure. MP377-P0-EXC-S1D and MP377-P0-T08F must use physical Codex plus Claude dogfood gates rather than tests alone. If Claude is not typed-attached or role-swap evidence is unavailable, the physical gate is blocked, not passed.

source_summary: The governed exception Slice 1 foundation cannot be called end-to-end accepted from tests alone. After the read-only EXC-S1 commands and registry projections are green, physically exercise the real devctl surfaces: exceptions pending, ex...
source_text: The governed exception Slice 1 foundation cannot be called end-to-end accepted from tests alone. After the read-only EXC-S1 commands and registry projections are green, physically exercise the real devctl surfaces: exceptions pending, exceptions validate with fixtures, platform-contracts, system-map, parser boundary proof that request is unreachable, and review-channel packet observation. Acceptance also requires live Codex plus Claude dogfood in both role assignments: Codex implementer with Claude reviewer, then Claude implementer with Codex reviewer. Record dogfood rows and check_multi_agent_sync after each phase. If a live provider is not typed-attached or role-swap evidence cannot be produced, the S1D acceptance gate is blocked, not passed.

source_text: The role/session packet inbox slice must be physically tested through the review-channel, not accepted from unit tests alone. Packets must route by target_role plus exact target_session_id when scoped, through typed CollaborationSession, SessionPosture, actor authority, and capability grants. Provider labels such as to_agent and from_agent are compatibility delivery labels only. The live dogfood must post packets, observe inbox/watch/current-session behavior, ACK or apply with evidence, and prove no packet disappears before disposition or typed plan/finding/receipt ingestion. Run both role assignments: Codex reviewer with Claude implementer and Claude reviewer with Codex implementer. Legacy target aliases such as codex or claude may work only when the role/session resolution is unambiguous. Ambiguous role routing fails closed. If the live peer/provider is unavailable, record the physical gate as blocked rather than accepted.

source_summary: Typed guard-deferral lifecycle future slice. Scope: add GuardDeferralReceipt or CheckDeferralReceipt plus QualityClosureReceipt for cleanup-grade guard debt only. Non-deferrable safety, proof, and authority gates cannot be deferred: secu...
source_text: Typed guard-deferral lifecycle future slice. Scope: add GuardDeferralReceipt or CheckDeferralReceipt plus QualityClosureReceipt for cleanup-grade guard debt only. Non-deferrable safety, proof, and authority gates cannot be deferred: security, raw bypass, exception execution exposure, public command exposure, generated markdown authority, missing TypedAction, missing remote-ref proof, missing PlanSourceSnapshot, missing authority contract registration, stale HEAD for mutation or publication claims, missing push proof, or failing slice acceptance tests. Deferrable checks are policy-classified only; deferral requires exact guard_id, target path or contract scope, reason, owner, follow-up PlanRow or FindingBacklog or AutomationDebt row, required rerun command, expiry, status=open, and typed visibility in SYSTEM_MAP or system-picture. Open guard deferrals are blockers: they must run and close before checkpoint, push, resolution, slice close, or success claims. Closure requires rerun proof and a QualityClosureReceipt. Repeated deferral escalates. This is future work and must not change MP377-P0-EXC-S1 behavior except to record the requirement durably.

source_summary: Codex process failure correction. Failure observed: operator architecture feedback about guard scheduling and deferral closure risk was initially treated as covered by a broad cadence plan/doc update instead of immediately creating or re...
source_text: Codex process failure correction. Failure observed: operator architecture feedback about guard scheduling and deferral closure risk was initially treated as covered by a broad cadence plan/doc update instead of immediately creating or refining a scoped typed PlanRow with a PlanIntentIngestionReceipt and PlanSourceSnapshot. Root cause: Codex optimized for summarizing and grouping related architecture prose, which can bury a new invariant inside huge plans and make it easy to lose before typed authority internalizes it. Required fix: when the operator introduces or corrects an architectural invariant, governance rule, acceptance gate, closure blocker, or critique of agent process, the agent must first classify whether it creates a new typed row, refines an existing typed row, or creates a terminal rejection/duplicate receipt. The agent must run develop ingest-plan or the current typed ingestion path before relying on markdown prose, chat memory, summaries, or generated docs. The receipt must preserve the full source text or a scoped source snapshot, link to the affected PlanRow ids, and include an explain-back of what changed and why. Documentation and broad plan prose may project the result only after the typed receipt exists. Acceptance: add guard/docs/process coverage so operator correction intake is not satisfied by broad prose alone; corrections about missed ingestion become typed quality/process debt; future closure reports cite receipt ids; no major governance feature can claim complete when an unresolved operator-correction intake row remains open for its scope.

source_summary: Operator architecture correction: AGENTS.md and Claude instruction surfaces are too large and behave like chat-prose dumps instead of generated routed indexes. Problem: huge instruction files can hide active rules, exceed practical AI at...
source_text: Operator architecture correction: AGENTS.md and Claude instruction surfaces are too large and behave like chat-prose dumps instead of generated routed indexes. Problem: huge instruction files can hide active rules, exceed practical AI attention, and cause agents to miss typed workflows. Required direction: generated AI instruction surfaces should be slim, bounded, and route agents to typed authority surfaces such as SYSTEM_MAP, active plan rows, devctl slash/command adapters, repo-pack policy, and generated role-specific bootstrap/context packets. AGENTS.md and Claude-facing equivalents must not become the durable home for all governance prose; they should summarize non-negotiable boot rules, point to typed state readers, and fail closed when generated projections are stale. Scope: identify whether AGENTS.md and Claude instruction files are generated or hand-edited; define the generator/source of truth; split long prose into typed contracts, maintainer docs, SYSTEM_MAP, command help, and role-specific generated snippets; add guards so generated instruction stubs stay under a bounded line/size budget and link to authoritative typed surfaces. Acceptance: plan rows and docs identify the source-of-truth generator; AGENTS.md and Claude instruction surfaces are projected from typed/source docs rather than ad hoc chat prose; slim stubs contain only startup-critical rules and commands; full governance detail moves to linked typed/docs surfaces; instruction-surface sync guards prevent drift; no major governance closure relies on reading a 2000-line instruction file.

source_text: Prove boot-card dogfood by registered agent identity plus role lane plus AuthoritySnapshot/SessionPosture/capability state, not by provider name. Claude and Codex are example runtimes only. If session-resume returns authority_result=blocked for coordination_resync_required or code-shape debt, record fail-closed dogfood evidence, continue only read-only reducers, and do not claim multi-agent automation is green. Future proof matrix covers Codex and Claude as implementer, reviewer, operator, and observer plus any future registered agent under the same typed lane rules; role switching and handoff must be recorded through typed session/authority state, review-channel packets, or plan rows; mutation requires TypedAction and proof requires ActionResult, RunRecord, or ValidationReceipt.

source_summary: Remote-control campaign plan for MP-377. Operator is remote all day; Claude is the dashboard/remote-control reviewer lane and Codex is the implementer/reviewer-coder lane. All coordination flows through typed state: startup-context, sess...
source_text: Remote-control campaign plan for MP-377. Operator is remote all day; Claude is the dashboard/remote-control reviewer lane and Codex is the implementer/reviewer-coder lane. All coordination flows through typed state: startup-context, session-resume, review-channel, agent-loop, develop campaign, plan rows, packets, receipts, and guards. No bridge/dashboard/generated markdown is authority. The campaign must fail closed when remote-control attachment is stale, interaction_mode/reviewer_mode drift, or packet debt blocks role proof. Closure keeps the four live blockers visible: governed push closure proof via devctl push --execute without bypass, cross-source drift on interaction_mode/reviewer_mode, Pass-C dogfood from AGENTS.md, and bounded packet carry-forward debt triage. Codex builds first under typed gates; Claude reviews/dashboard-triages through CLAUDE.md and the review channel; then roles switch only when typed authority grants it. Raw bypass is not introduced; publication remains devctl push --execute with remote-ref proof.
